mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, enabling users to build, deploy, and manage machine learning workflows. It is ideal for AI practitioners and platform administrators looking for a modular and scalable solution for their AI projects. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is essential for managing Kubeflow deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to the Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kubeflow apply --version v1.10.0

```

> Deploy Kubeflow version 1.10.0.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Kubeflow.
> Enable autoscaling for the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubeflow delete --version v1.10.0

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Remove Kubeflow from the cluster.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubeflow upgrade --version v1.10.0

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of all pods before upgrading.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'gcloud components install kubectl' or download it from the Kubernetes website.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check node resources with 'kubectl get nodes' and scale the cluster if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Kubeflow CLI is installed and added to your PATH.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI lifecycle components, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate your existing pipelines and configurations as per the migration guide.

resolution.summary: You should see all nodes in 'Ready' state and the Kubeflow dashboard accessible via the provided URL.

prerequisites.resources: Minimum 3 nodes with 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM each
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GCP account and the necessary permissions to create Kubernetes clusters.
